articles.title: 32,000 People Displaced By Philippine Earthquake
articles.content:
菲律宾桑托斯将军城（美联社）——周二，救援人员在菲律宾南部搜寻被毁建筑，确认是否仍有人被困。一天前，该国遭遇半个世纪以来最强烈地震之一，造成至少37人死亡，超过3.2万人流离失所。

官方记录显示，周一上午7.8级地震震中附近的南部省份仅有4人失踪，但民防办公室承认，必须彻底检查数座倒塌和严重受损的建筑，寻找可能的幸存者或伤亡人员。

震中位于菲律宾第二大岛棉兰老岛附近，近500人受伤，超过3.2万人流离失所，大多数人逃往紧急避难所。

许多逃离家园的居民担心海啸。菲律宾测得高达1.4米（4.6英尺）的海浪，但据报告的唯一海啸损失是沿海村庄六间高脚棚屋。较小的海浪在印度尼西亚、帕劳乃至日本南部海岸登陆。

山体滑坡和建筑物倒塌导致多人死亡。

地震造成广泛破坏，包括在桑托斯将军城——这个拥有70多万人口的繁华沿海城市，被誉为该国的金枪鱼之都，至少有13人在建筑倒塌和坠落碎片中丧生。

据民防办公室的拉斐尔·亚历杭德罗称，沙兰岸省至少18人死亡，其中大部分是在格兰镇发生的山体滑坡中，滑坡掩埋了山区房屋。

灾害应对官员表示，其他死亡病例报告来自南哥打巴托省和达沃 Occidental省，以及巴卢特岛。

根据政府初步损失评估，数省约2500栋房屋和117座政府建筑及设施受损。桑托斯将军城国际机场连续第二天关闭，除人道主义任务航班外，63架次国内航班被迫取消。

地震受灾省份约6000栋公立学校建筑必须在复课前接受评估。地震发生在全国暑假两个月后开学第一天，许多受伤者是兴奋地参加早晨升旗仪式的年轻学生。

当局警告，出现裂缝的建筑可能因余震而倒塌，其中一些余震威力巨大。

亚历杭德罗说：“我们不能强制学校立即重新开放，因为必须确保建筑的完整性。”

周一上午发生的地震震源位于海底33公里（20英里）深处，棉兰老岛西南约32公里（20英里）处的沙兰岸省马西姆镇附近。

菲律宾火山与地震研究所所长特雷西托·巴科尔科尔表示，这次地震由科塔巴托海沟的移动引发，是自1976年以来最强的地震。当时同一海底凹陷引发了8.1级地震，激起海啸。

巴科尔科尔说，那次地震和海啸造成约8000人死亡，高达8至10米（26至33英尺）的海浪席卷了多个城镇和省份。

巴科尔科尔告诉美联社，菲律宾地震研究所计划在8月纪念1976年地震和海啸周年纪念日，通过安装标记提醒脆弱城镇和城市保持警惕。

1990年发生的7.8级地震造成1000多人死亡，数千人受伤，北部省份和城市遭受大面积破坏。

马科斯总统派遣顶级国防减灾官员从马尼拉前往监督搜救工作，协调向地震灾民分发数万个食品包和建筑材料，并评估桥梁、道路和其他基础设施的损失。

美国作为菲律宾的条约盟友表示正在与马尼拉协调，准备支持菲律宾的应对工作。法国、日本和新西兰也表达了支持。

菲律宾位于太平洋“火环”，是环绕海洋的地震断层弧，因此经常遭受地震和火山喷发。

该群岛每年还遭受约20个台风和热带风暴的袭击，使其成为世界上最易受灾的国家之一。

____

戈麦斯发自菲律宾马尼拉。美联社记者巴西利奥·塞佩在菲律宾桑托斯将军城、哈鲁卡·努加在曼谷参与报道。
articles.summary:
GENERAL SANTOS, Philippines (AP) — Rescuers searched ruined buildings in the southern Philippines on Tuesday to ensure no one was still trapped a day after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country in a half-century killed at least 37 people and displaced more than 32,000.

Only four people were considered missing on official records in the southern provinces near where the 7.8 magnitude quake struck Monday morning, but the Office of Civil Defense acknowledged several collapsed and heavily damaged buildings must be thoroughly inspected for possible survivors or casualties.

Advertisement

The earthquake centered off Mindanao, the second most populous Philippine island, injured nearly 500 people and displaced more than 32,000, most of whom fled to emergency shelters.

Many people who left their homes feared a tsunami. Waves up to 1.4-meters (4.6-feet) above tide level were measured in the Philippines, but the only tsunami damage reported was to six shanties on stilts in a coastal village. Smaller waves washed ashore in Indonesia and Palau and as far away as southern Japan.

Landslides and building collapses caused several deaths

The earthquake left a trail of destruction, including in General Santos, a lively coastal city of more than 700,000 people known as the country’s tuna capital, where at least 13 people were killed in collapsed buildings and due to falling debris.

Advertisement

At least 18 died in Sarangani province, mostly in a landslide that buried houses in the mountainside town of Glan, according to Rafaelito Alejandro of the Office of Civil Defense.

The other deaths were reported in the southern provinces of South Cotabato and Davao Occidental, and on Balut Island, disaster response officials said.

About 2,500 houses and 117 government buildings and facilities were damaged in several provinces, according to an initial government damage assessment. The international airport in General Santos remained shut for a second day, forcing the cancellation of 63 domestic flights except for those on humanitarian missions.

Advertisement

About 6,000 public school buildings in quake-hit provinces must be assessed before classes can resume. The quake struck on the first day of classes nationwide after a two-month summer break, and many who sustained injuries were young students who had gathered with excitement for morning flag-raising ceremonies.

Authorities have warned that buildings that sustained cracks could collapse due to aftershocks, some of them dangerously powerful.

“We cannot force the immediate reopening of schools because we have to ensure the integrity of the buildings,” Alejandro said.

Advertisement

It was the strongest Philippine quake since 1976

Monday’s earthquake was centered at sea at a depth of 33 kilometers (20 miles), about 32 kilometers (20 miles) southwest of Maasim town in Sarangani province.

It was set off by movement in the Cotabato Trench and was the strongest since the same undersea depression triggered an 8.1-magnitude quake that whipped up tsunami waves on Aug. 17, 1976, said Teresito Bacolcol, the director of the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology.

About 8,000 people died from that quake and tsunami waves of up to 8 to 10 meters (26 to 33 feet) that engulfed several towns and provinces, Bacolcol said.

Advertisement

The Philippine seismological institute was scheduled to commemorate the anniversary of the 1976 quake and tsunami in August by installing markers to remind vulnerable towns and cities of the need for constant vigilance, Bacolcol told The Associated Press.

A 1990 earthquake that also had a magnitude of 7.8 left more than 1,000 people dead, injured thousands and caused extensive damages in northern provinces and cities.

President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. deployed top defense-mitigation officials from Manila to help oversee search and rescue, the distribution of tens of thousands of food packs and construction materials to quake victims and assess damage to bridges, roads and other infrastructure.

Advertisement

The United States, a treaty ally of the Philippines, said it was coordinating with Manila and was ready to support Philippine response efforts. France, Japan and New Zealand also expressed support.

The Philippines is often hit by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions due to its location on the Pacific “Ring of Fire,” an arc of seismic faults around the ocean.

The archipelago is also battered by about 20 typhoons and tropical storms each year, making it one of the world’s most disaster-prone countries.

Advertisement

___

Gomez reported from Manila, Philippines. AP journalists Basilio Sepe in General Santos, Philippines, and Haruka Nuga in Bangkok contributed to this report.

Related
PhilippinesEarthquakes

Adblock test (Why?)

articles.title: Congo's Ebola Outbreak Rises To 100 Deaths Out Of 550 Cases As Conflict Slows Response
articles.content:
布尼亚，刚果（美联社）——当局表示，刚果东部宣布埃博拉疫情不到一个月，至少已有100人死于埃博拉。这一严峻的死亡数字出现之际，官员们正加紧努力控制这场被发现延误数周的疫情。

5月15日宣布的此次疫情持续受到多方面挑战：愤怒居民袭击卫生工作者、部分当地人的怀疑态度，以及热点地区的武装冲突。疫情由一种严重的埃博拉疾病形式引起。

根据周一晚间的最新情况报告，截至周日，已确认550例埃博拉病例，其中101人死亡，19人康复。疫情主要集中在刚果东部伊图里省，该省病例占90%以上。北基伍省和南基伍省也出现了病例，疫情已蔓延至邻国乌干达。

由于疫情确认延误数周，且接触者追踪覆盖率虽近几天有所改善但仍仅为64%，刚果的实际病例数被认为更高。

最新埃博拉疫情由罕见的布迪布迪奥病毒引起，与导致刚果过去16次疫情的“扎伊尔病毒”（即埃博拉病毒的别称）不同，目前尚无经批准的疫苗或治疗方法。

当局表示，病例快速增加部分原因是诊断能力扩大，使此前采集积压样本的检测成为可能。

**疫情扰乱省会日常生活**

为限制埃博拉传播而采取的卫生措施扰乱了伊图里省繁忙省会布尼亚的日常生活。

“当局要求我们不能再用同一辆摩托车载两位乘客。现在每辆摩托车只允许载一位乘客。”摩托车司机贾斯汀·阿贝卡尼说。

该省部分地区仍普遍存在对卫生协议的怀疑和漠视。刚果2018年埃博拉疫情（历史上第二大埃博拉疫情）的幸存者警告说，重蹈覆辙可能导致大量本可预防的死亡。

一线卫生工作者薪酬微薄、得不到休息，多次遭到愤怒居民袭击，由于涉及武装叛乱团体的冲突，无法抵达部分社区。

东部刚果多年来遭受数十个独立叛乱和武装组织的袭击，其中一些与外国或极端组织“伊斯兰国”有关联。

**冲突与人口流动使疾病追踪复杂化**

世界卫生组织周一表示，冲突“限制了应对工作的进入，扰乱了监测和应对活动，并增加了未检测到传播的风险”。“此类事件凸显了环境背景的挑战，以及与当地领导和社区密切合作的重要性。”

据联合国人道主义事务办公室称，伊图里省已有近100万人因冲突流离失所，这使得接触者追踪工作变得困难，因为人们逃离袭击或在广阔省份中频繁流动。该省森林茂密、道路状况差、偏远村庄可能需要数天才能到达。

在矿产丰富的地区，在数千名经常往返于偏远矿点的个体矿工中进行追踪同样困难。

世卫组织目前评估疫情蔓延至非洲其他地区和全球的风险为低。

世卫组织总干事谭德塞博士周一在访问乌干达时表示：“如果（埃博拉）患者获得所需医疗支持，是可以康复的。”

——阿萨杜发自尼日利亚阿布贾

——更多非洲和发展资讯：https://apnews.com/hub/africa-pulse

美联社获得比尔及梅琳达·盖茨基金会对非洲全球健康与发展报道的财务支持。美联社对所有内容负全部责任。请在AP.org查阅美联社与慈善机构合作的标准、支持者名单及资助报道领域。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BUNIA, Congo (AP) — At least 100 people have died from Ebola less than a month after authorities declared an outbreak of the disease in eastern Congo, authorities said, a grim toll as officials intensify efforts to slow the disease discovered weeks late.

Attacks on health workers from angry residents, skepticism among some locals and armed conflict in hot spots continue to challenge efforts to stop the outbreak declared on May 15, which has been caused by a severe form of Ebola disease.

Advertisement

Out of the 550 cases of the disease confirmed as of Sunday, there have been 101 deaths and 19 recoveries, according to the latest situation report late Monday. The outbreak is concentrated in Congo’s eastern province of Ituri, which accounts for more than 90% of the cases. Cases have also been recorded in the North Kivu and South Kivu provinces, and has spread across the border to Uganda.

The number of cases in Congo is believed to be higher because the outbreak was confirmed weeks late and the contact tracing coverage rate, which has improved in recent days, is still at 64%.

Advertisement

The latest Ebola disease outbreak is caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, which does not have an approved vaccine or treatment unlike the “Zaire virus,” another name for the Ebola virus, responsible for most of Congo’s past 16 outbreaks of the disease.

The rapid increase in the number of cases is in part due to the scale up of diagnostic capacities, enabling testing of the backlog of previously collected samples, authorities said.

Capital of Ituri province disrupted by outbreak

Health measures put in place to limit the spread of Ebola have disrupted daily life in Bunia, the bustling capital of Ituri province.

Advertisement

“The authorities have asked us not to carry two customers on the same motorbike anymore. We are now only allowed to carry one customer per motorbike,” motorcyclist Justin Abekani said.

There is still widespread skepticism and disregard for health protocols in some parts of the province. Survivors of Congo’s 2018 Ebola outbreak, the second-biggest in history, have warned that a repeat of past mistakes could lead to a high number of preventable deaths.

Advertisement

Front line health workers, with little pay or rest, have been attacked multiple times by angry residents and have not been able to reach some communities due conflict involving armed rebel groups.

Eastern Congo has for years seen attacks by dozens of separate rebel and militant groups, some of them with links to foreign countries or to the extremist Islamic State group.

Conflict and movement complicate disease tracing

Conflict is “constraining access for the response, disrupting surveillance and response activities, and increasing the risk of undetected transmission,” the World Health Organization said Monday. “Such incidents underline the challenges of the context and the importance of working closely with local leaders and communities,” WHO added.

Advertisement

Nearly a million people have been displaced by conflict in Ituri, according to the U.N. humanitarian office, making contact tracing difficult as people flee attacks or move frequently in the vast province with dense forests, poor roads and remote villages that can take days to reach.

Tracing also is difficult among the thousands of artisanal miners who regularly move between remote sites in the mineral-rich region.

Advertisement

WHO currently asseses the risk of spread for the rest of Africa and at the global level as low.

“(Ebola) patients can recover if they get the medical support they need,” WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said Monday during a visit to Uganda.

___

Asadu reported from Abuja, Nigeria.

___

For more on Africa and development: https://apnews.com/hub/africa-pulse

The Associated Press receives financial support for global health and development coverage in Africa from the Gates Foundation. The AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP’s standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.
